aiashley — global digest Generated 09:47 UTC

Monday, May 18, 2026

International News · 16 Sources · 4 Regions

Daily Brief

The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo a public health emergency of international concern on Sunday, with approximately 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths from a rare Bundibugyo strain for which no approved vaccine exists [BBC], [Globe and Mail]. The virus has already spread to Uganda, raising fears of regional transmission. Meanwhile, G7 finance ministers convene in Paris today amid sharp disagreements over trade policy, China relations, and the economic fallout from the ongoing US-Israel war with Iran [RFI], [Le Monde]. That conflict showed no signs of resolution as President Trump warned Iran the 'clock is ticking' after Tehran rejected US demands as excessive [BBC], [France 24]. A drone strike on a nuclear power plant in Abu Dhabi underscored the fragility of the ceasefire, with no group claiming responsibility [RNZ], [Globe and Mail]. In a significant development for US-China relations, Beijing agreed to purchase $17 billion annually in US agricultural products following the Trump-Xi summit, offering relief to American farmers caught in the trade war [The Hindu]. Japanese government bond yields hit 29-year highs at 2.8% as fiscal concerns mount amid the Middle East crisis [Nikkei Asia].

Top Stories
01 Health

WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in DR Congo a Global Health Emergency

  • The WHO declared the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo's Ituri province a public health emergency of international concern, citing approximately 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths
  • The outbreak involves the rare Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, for which no approved vaccines or treatments currently exist
  • The virus has spread beyond Congo's borders, with two confirmed cases in Uganda including one fatality in Kampala
  • Health officials warn the outbreak may be 'potentially much larger' than detected, having circulated undetected for at least four weeks in a region marked by mining activity and armed conflict
Coverage
BBC WorldEmphasized the technical aspects of the WHO declaration and provided detailed epidemiological context The GuardianEarlier reporting focused on initial case counts before WHO declaration Globe and MailHighlighted the lack of approved treatments and the speed of WHO's response without usual procedures
02 Economics

G7 Finance Ministers Meet in Paris Amid Deep Divisions on Trade and Iran War Fallout

  • Finance ministers and central bankers from G7 nations began two-day talks in Paris on Monday to address divergent positions on trade, China policy, and economic disruptions from the Iran conflict
  • France, holding the G7 presidency, acknowledged significant disagreements with the US over new 15 percent tariff surcharges imposed earlier this year
  • Discussions will address America's budget deficit, Europe's innovation challenges, Chinese industrial overcapacity, and critical mineral supply chains
  • French Finance Minister Roland Lescure stated 'the way the global economy has been developing for more than a decade is clearly unsustainable'
Coverage
RFI EnglishDetailed coverage of French hosting role and specific policy disagreements Le MondeArticle title available but full content not accessible
03 Conflict

Trump Warns Iran as Ceasefire Talks Stall; Drone Strikes Hit Gulf States

  • President Trump warned Iran the 'clock is ticking' after Tehran rejected US ceasefire demands as excessive, with talks continuing through Pakistani mediation
  • Iran announced formation of a new body to manage the Strait of Hormuz, which Tehran has effectively closed and seeks to charge ships to traverse
  • Drone strikes hit targets in Saudi Arabia and the UAE on Sunday, including a fire at the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in Abu Dhabi; no casualties or radiation release reported
  • Iranian media reported negotiations at an 'impasse' while Trump said Iran was 'dying to sign' a deal but failing to honor terms
Coverage
BBC WorldBalanced presentation of both US and Iranian positions on negotiations France 24Emphasized ongoing diplomatic exchanges and Iranian response to US proposals RNZFocused on the nuclear plant attack and UAE security response Globe and MailConnected attack to broader ceasefire fragility and regional tensions The HinduDetailed Iranian announcement of Strait of Hormuz management body
04 Economics

China Agrees to $17 Billion Annual US Agricultural Purchases Following Trump-Xi Summit

  • China committed to purchasing US agricultural products at an annualized rate of $17 billion per year for 2026-2028, the White House announced Sunday
  • The deal includes restored market access for US beef and resumed poultry imports from bird-flu-free states
  • The agreement follows President Trump's return from a summit in Beijing aimed at easing trade war impacts on American farmers
  • American farmers face additional pressure from the Iran war, which has restricted global fertilizer supplies through the Strait of Hormuz and driven up prices
Coverage
The HinduProvided detailed terms of the agreement and noted Beijing had not immediately confirmed the specifics
05 Economics

Japan Bond Yields Hit 29-Year High as Fiscal and Inflation Concerns Mount

  • Ten-year Japanese government bond yields climbed to 2.8% on Monday, the highest level since October 1996
  • Investors are selling sovereign debt amid inflation pressures and fiscal concerns exacerbated by the Middle East conflict
  • The Japanese government has begun deliberating whether to compile a supplementary budget to address rising fuel costs
  • The yen dropped to its lowest level since the last currency intervention
Coverage
Nikkei AsiaConnected bond market moves to broader Middle East conflict disruptions and domestic policy considerations
Also Today
  • US and Nigerian forces killed Islamic State's global second-in-command Abu-Bilal al-Minuki in joint operation The Guardian
  • Ukrainian drones kill three in large-scale overnight attack on Moscow region, Russia reports BBC Europe
  • California jury begins deliberations in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI over nonprofit mission breach France 24
  • Amnesty International reports global executions at highest level since 1981, driven by surge in Iran Le Monde
  • France's Justice Minister visits Algeria to discuss judicial cooperation and detained journalist Christophe Gleizes France 24
  • Canal+ chief announces company will no longer work with 600 industry professionals who signed anti-Bolloré petition RFI English
  • Mali's military junta launches airstrikes against rebel alliance as Russian mercenaries support counteroffensive The Guardian
  • Magnitude 5.2 earthquake in China's Guangxi region kills two and forces 7,000 evacuations The Hindu
  • Ukraine approaches New Zealand about partnership to build military drones RNZ
  • Shai Gilgeous-Alexander named NBA MVP for second consecutive season RFI English
